summary: Gruber performs a line-by-line satirical translation of Carrier IQ's corporate press release, exposing the gap between its sanitized PR language and the reality of its hidden smartphone surveillance software. The post systematically deconstructs each paragraph of Carrier IQ's 'Media Alert,' replacing euphemisms with blunt descriptions of what the company actually does. Gruber highlights that Carrier IQ's software was secretly embedded on phones, could record keystrokes despite denials, and that its carrier customers would happily sell the collected data to third parties. The piece is structured as a call-and-response between the original corporate text and Gruber's translations, building to an increasingly dismissive and comedic tone.
key_points: Carrier IQ's PR language about 'Mobile Intelligence' and 'operational information' is designed to obscure that it's logging detailed device usage without consumer knowledge
Despite explicitly denying keystroke recording, Gruber links to evidence showing Carrier IQ's software can in fact record keystrokes and track users
The company deflects responsibility to carriers and device manufacturers who embed the software, while those same customers sell personal data to third parties
Corporate jargon like 'mission critical tool' and 'measurable impact' serves as a smokescreen for surveillance technology consumers never consented to
money_quotes: We Got Caught Doing Something We Were Kind of Hoping No One Would Notice and Now We're Nervously Placing Exclamation Marks at the End of Our Media Alert Headlines!
Carrier IQ provides handset makers and mobile carriers with detailed device usage logs that consumers are never made aware of. We capitalized "Mobile Intelligence" because that makes it even smarter.
Come on people, it's just "operational information".
If the carriers and handset makers who include our software want it there, it must be OK. Nothing to look at here. Ignore that man behind the curtain.
Our software can be used to record keystrokes and provides tracking tools.
Our customers — phone carriers — will sell personal subscriber information to third parties.
Trust us. We've had this shit audited. Can't you see that we are serious?
key_insight: Corporate PR doublespeak about 'mobile intelligence' and 'user experience' is a thin veneer over secret consumer surveillance that the company hopes no one will bother to decode.
